rulings.Case Title: LONNETTE ROBINSON ET AL VS. LARUE PERKINS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Dissolving Partnership And Appointing Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 17, 2023 line 1. PETITIONER KERRY BOWSER Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Dissolving Partnership And Appointing Receiver is continued to April 14, 2023, at 9:30. No later than March 31, 2023, each party to file a supplemental brief (not to exceed 2 pages) to address whether this dispute should be settled by arbitration, pursuant to Article VI, section 6.02 of the Partnership Agreement. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TAL N. TAMIR ET AL VS. LEM HOLDINGS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO ENFORCE COURT'S ORDER AND FOR ISSUE, EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS AGAINST LEM HOLDINGS, LLC, CIARAN HARTY AND PATRICK HARTY
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 17, 2023 line 3. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO ENFORCE COURT'S ORDER AND FOR ISSUE, EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS AGAINST LEM HOLDINGS, LLC, CIARAN HARTY AND PATRICK HARTY is GRANTED and shall be entered nunc pro tunc November 15, 2022. The Court considered the original opposition, declaration of Mr. Joyce, and parts of the amended opposition authorized by March 9, 2023 order. The Court notes that the amended opposition was filed in violation of the Court's order disregarding its express instructions. This is indicative of the defendant's disregard for this Court's orders, including discovery orders. Plaintiff to prepare an order repeating sections 1 and 2 of the November 15, 2022 order. (It appears that sections 3 and 4 re: monetary sanctions have been complied with). On the issue of sanctions associated with the motion for relief from November 15, 2023 order: there will be no sanctions awarded. Motion for Summary Adjudication will proceed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARK SMITH VS. JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 17, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANT JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is CONTINUED to April 14, 2023 for supplemental briefing to address whether this motion is moot in light of the filing of the first amended complaint. rulings.Case Title: ARDEN INTENGAN VS. C.N.A. FORECLOSURE SERVICES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 17, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANT C.N.A. FORECLOSURE SERVICES, INC. DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part, and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained, with leave to amend, as to negligence, UNRUH, and breach of contract claims, and overruled as to the accounting claim. The Negligence claim was unsupported by facts that establish the element of duty for negligence. The UNRUH claim was unsupported by facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action-Plaintiff only makes conclusory allegations. The breach of contract claim was unsupported by a copy of the contract to its pleading, and the First Amended Complaint did not specify the terms of the contract. The accounting claim is overruled. Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint states facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. (Code Civ. Proc., 430.10 subd. (e).)=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ONSITE PROPERTY SYSTEMS VS. KRYSTYNA SEREDA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 17, 2023 line 7. PLAINTIFF ONSITE PROPERTY SYSTEMS Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment is DENIED, without prejudice. The Declaration of Brandy Stinson does not explain how she knows that the payments were not made. The Court notes that the late filed declaration of Greg Pennington does not establish defendants presently have adequate funds to bring rent arrears current; that opposition was served by mail in violation of CCP 1005(c). Opposition was considered. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
